title: How to Make Fire
content: Use the bow drill method: find a dry softwood fireboard and spindle. Apply downward pressure and friction to generate an ember, then transfer to a tinder bundle and blow gently.

title: Building a Temporary Shelter
content: Find a natural windbreak. Lean branches against a fallen log at 45 degrees. Layer smaller sticks and leaves on top for insulation. Keep it small to retain body heat.

title: Filtering Water
content: Layer a container with grass, fine sand, crushed charcoal, and small rocks. Pour water through slowly. Always boil the filtered water before drinking if possible.

title: Navigation Without GPS
content: Use the shadow-tip method during daytime. At night follow the North Star (Polaris). Moss tends to grow thicker on the north side of trees in the northern hemisphere.

title: Basic First Aid in the Wild
content: Clean wounds with purified water. Use plantain leaves as a poultice for minor cuts. Immobilize suspected fractures with a splint made from straight sticks and cloth.
